mnemonics: LOCK Before You Rock (a transaction) - Emphasizes that acquiring a lock is a prerequisite for performing an operation to ensure serializability.
Two-Phase Rule: GROW Before you SHRINK - A transaction must acquire all its locks (growing phase) before it starts releasing any (shrinking phase).
keyPoints: Validation (Optimistic Concurrency Control) assumes conflicts are rare and checks for them at the end of a transaction during a 'validation phase'.
Lock-Based (Pessimistic) protocols prevent conflicts by requiring transactions to acquire locks on data items before operating on them.
The most common timestamp ordering protocol uses the timestamps of transactions to determine the serializability order.
commonMistakes: Assuming that just any locking protocol ensures serializability. For example, simple locking without the two-phase rule does not.
Confusing the 'validation' phase in optimistic methods with the 'voting' phase in two-phase commit, which are different concepts for different purposes.
Believing a read operation never requires a lock. Shared (read) locks are often needed to prevent other transactions from writing the same data.
